file_name: GWG HOLDINGS, INC. - ORDERLY MARKETING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.2 It is the goal of the Seller Trusts to have all of the Shares sold, on a pro-rata basis, in three or more tranches (each a "Tranche") commencing not earlier than six (6) months after the Effective Date with the resale of all of the shares of Stock completed as soon as practicable after the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 It is the goal of the Seller Trusts to have all of the Shares sold, on a pro-rata basis, in three or more tranches (each a "Tranche") commencing not earlier than six (6) months after the Effective Date with the resale of all of the shares of Stock completed within 12 months of the Effective Date. However, The Seller Trusts reserve the right to retain unsold shares indefinitely, at their sole discretion, regardless of the set time period.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating a goal to complete the sale of shares within 12 months, while simultaneously allowing the Seller Trusts to retain unsold shares indefinitely. This contradicts the fixed timeframe, creating uncertainty about the actual duration and intent of the share sale process. It contradicts itself when it says 'completed within 12 months' and then 'retain unsold shares indefinitely'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is the goal of the Seller Trusts to have all of the Shares sold, on a pro-rata basis, in three or more tranches (each a "Tranche") commencing not earlier than six (6) months after the Effective Date with the resale of all of the shares of Stock completed within 12 months of the Effective Date. However, The Seller Trusts reserve the right to retain unsold shares indefinitely, at their sole discretion, regardless of the set time period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. It initially states that the sale of shares should be completed within 12 months of the Effective Date. However, it then adds that the Seller Trusts can retain unsold shares indefinitely, regardless of the set time period. This directly conflicts with the stated goal of completing the sale within 12 months, creating a clear inconsistency. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction: '...when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.'

perturbation.original_text: 1.5 After the Parties have agreed on the strategy for the marketing, sale and distribution of a Tranche, the Seller Trusts shall offer the Bank the right to serve as the lead left joint-book-running manager in connection with a best efforts distribution. GWG shall be entitled to appoint, in its discretion, an additional bank as joint book-running manager to participate in the distribution.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 After the Parties have agreed on the strategy for the marketing, sale and distribution of a Tranche, the Seller Trusts may or may not offer the Bank the right to serve as the lead left joint-book-running manager in connection with a best efforts distribution. GWG shall forfeit its right to appoint an additional bank as joint book-running manager to participate in the distribution.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by changing the obligatory 'shall offer' to 'may or may not offer,' making the offering of the bank's services discretionary. It also removes GWG's right to appoint an additional bank ('shall forfeit its right') which conflicts with the initial agreement that gives it the right. This creates uncertainty as to whether the Bank's services will actually be offered and what are GWG's rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: After the Parties have agreed on the strategy for the marketing, sale and distribution of a Tranche, the Seller Trusts may or may not offer the Bank the right to serve as the lead left joint-book-running manager in connection with a best efforts distribution. GWG shall forfeit its right to appoint an additional bank as joint book-running manager to participate in the distribution. The Seller Trusts further agree that in the event the Bank accepts such role it will be paid customary fees for the performance of its services in connection with such transactions and that such engagement will involve the execution of a standard form agreement with respect to the distribution of each Tranche, which may be in the form of a placement agency agreement, underwriting agreement or other appropriate agreement (each, a "Distribution Agreement"); provided, however, that nothing contained in this OMA or Engagement Letter shall require the Bank to underwrite or purchase all or any portion of a Tranche of Stock for its own account. Notwithstanding the foregoing, it is understood and agreed that the Bank or its affiliates may, solely at its discretion and without any obligation to do so, purchase Stock in any Tranche as principal.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, which states 'may or may not offer' and removes GWG's right, directly contradicts the original agreement by making the bank's involvement discretionary and eliminating GWG's appointment rights. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, which indicates a complete replacement of the original clause that ensured the bank would be offered the role and GWG had appointment rights. This creates an in-text contradiction as the previous obligations and entitlements are now uncertain or nullified, resulting in conflicting interpretations of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Term; Termination; Withdrawal of Bank. (a) This OMA shall expire upon the earlier of (i) the first anniversary of the Effective Date and (ii) the date that all Shares of Stock of the Seller Trusts as set forth on Schedule A hereto have been sold (the "Term"). Notwithstanding the foregoing, this OMA may be terminated with or without cause at any time after the Effective Date and without liability or continuing obligation by any of the Parties hereto (i) by mutual written agreement of all of the Parties; and (ii) in writing by the Trust Advisors in their sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Term; Termination; Withdrawal of Bank. (a) This OMA shall expire upon the earlier of (i) the first anniversary of the Effective Date and (ii) the date that all Shares of Stock of the Seller Trusts as set forth on Schedule A hereto have been sold (the "Term"). Notwithstanding the foregoing, this OMA may only be terminated with cause at any time after the Effective Date, creating liability and continuing obligation by any of the Parties hereto (i) only by mutual written agreement of all of the Parties; and (ii) only in writing by GWG's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The change to 'may only be terminated with cause' contradicts the original 'may be terminated with or without cause.' Additionally, changing 'without liability or continuing obligation' to 'creating liability and continuing obligation' reverses the consequences of termination. Furthermore, taking termination power from 'Trust Advisors' to 'GWG's sole discretion' contradicts the initial agreement and is contradictory. This leads to confusion about the conditions and consequences of terminating the OMA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This OMA shall expire upon the earlier of (i) the first anniversary of the Effective Date and (ii) the date that all Shares of Stock of the Seller Trusts as set forth on Schedule A hereto have been sold (the "Term"). Notwithstanding the foregoing, this OMA may only be terminated with cause at any time after the Effective Date, creating liability and continuing obligation by any of the Parties hereto (i) only by mutual written agreement of all of the Parties; and (ii) only in writing by GWG's sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text fundamentally alters the termination conditions of the OMA. The original text allows termination 'with or without cause' and 'without liability', whereas the changed text requires termination 'with cause' and imposes 'liability'. This is a direct contradiction. The change also removes the 'Trust Advisors' right to terminate and gives this right to 'GWG's sole discretion'. Since the contradicted text is now exactly the same as the changed text, this is also a direct contradiction.
